summary.L1_headline: + CYBR – Record Q4 and Full Year 2025 Results. | 02/04/26
summary.L2_brief: CyberArk Software Ltd. filed a 6-K on February 4, 2026, announcing record fourth quarter and full year 2025 results via press release. The U.S. GAAP financial information from the consolidated balance sheets, statements of operations, and cash flows was incorporated by reference into the Company's Registration Statements on Form S-8. Specific financial figures were not included in the 6-K body itself, as details are contained in the attached Exhibit 99.1 press release.

L3_detailed.event: CyberArk announced record fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results via a press release furnished as Exhibit 99.1 to this Form 6-K, dated February 4, 2026.
key_figures: Record Q4 2025 results (specific figures in Exhibit 99.1, not reproduced in 6-K body)
Record full year 2025 results (specific figures in Exhibit 99.1, not reproduced in 6-K body)
L3_detailed.market_impact: CyberArk trades on NASDAQ under ticker CYBR. The announcement of record quarterly and annual results is material information for US-listed shares. The U.S. GAAP financials were incorporated by reference into multiple Form S-8 registration statements.
L3_detailed.regulatory_notes: Filing made with the SEC pursuant to Rule 13a-16 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. The press release is furnished (not filed) under Section 18 of the Exchange Act, except for the U.S. GAAP financial statements which are incorporated by reference into Form S-8 registration statements.
L3_detailed.cross_border_considerations: CyberArk is incorporated in Israel with principal offices in Petach-Tikva, Israel. The company files annual reports on Form 20-F as a foreign private issuer. US investors hold ADR/ordinary shares listed on US exchanges. Reporting currency details are contained in Exhibit 99.1.
risks_flagged: Specific financial details are in Exhibit 99.1 which was not included in the parsed text; full analysis requires review of that exhibit

summary.L1_headline: + CYBR – deregisters all remaining unsold shares across 15 S-8 registration statements following acquisition by Palo Alto Networks. | 02/11/26
summary.L2_brief: CyberArk Software Ltd. filed Post-Effective Amendment No. 1 to 15 existing S-8 registration statements to deregister all remaining unsold ordinary shares previously registered under its 2001 Stock Option Plans, 2011 Share Option Plan, 2014 Share Incentive Plan, 2020 Employee Share Purchase Plan, and 2024 Share Incentive Plan. The deregistration follows the completion on February 11, 2026 of CyberArk's merger with a wholly owned subsidiary of Palo Alto Networks, Inc. (PANW), pursuant to a Merger Agreement dated July 30, 2025, resulting in CyberArk becoming a wholly owned subsidiary of PANW. All offerings of CyberArk securities under these registration statements have been terminated.

L3_detailed.shares_registered: Deregistration of all remaining unsold shares across 15 S-8 filings that originally registered a combined total of approximately 18,531,136 ordinary shares (1,951,230 + 2,467,885 + 224,000 + 219,358 + 610,027 + 3,311,644 + 1,200,000 + 1,300,000 + 50,000 + 125,000 + 1,225,000 + 1,100,000 + 200,000 + 1,100,000 + 150,000 + 1,786,992 + 1,480,000 + 30,000). The exact number of shares remaining unsold and now being deregistered is not specified.
L3_detailed.plan_name: Cyber-Ark Software Ltd. 2001 Section 102 Stock Option Plan; Cyber-Ark Software Ltd. 2001 Stock Option Plan; Cyber-Ark Software Ltd. 2011 Share Option Plan; CyberArk Software Ltd. 2014 Share Incentive Plan; CyberArk Software Ltd. 2020 Employee Share Purchase Plan; CyberArk Software Ltd. 2024 Share Incentive Plan
L3_detailed.securities_type: Ordinary shares, par value NIS 0.01 per share
L3_detailed.offering_price_basis: N/A — this filing deregisters securities; no new offering
L3_detailed.dilution_impact: N/A — shares are being removed from registration, not offered; no additional dilution
L3_detailed.deal_context: On February 11, 2026, Palo Alto Networks, Inc. (PANW) completed its acquisition of CyberArk Software Ltd. pursuant to the Merger Agreement dated July 30, 2025. Athens Strategies Ltd., a wholly owned Israeli subsidiary of PANW, merged with and into CyberArk, with CyberArk surviving as a wholly owned subsidiary of PANW. As a result, CyberArk terminated all offerings of its securities under the 15 Registration Statements and is deregistering all remaining unsold shares. The agent for service is Bruce Byrd, EVP and General Counsel of Palo Alto Networks, and the filing is signed from Santa Clara, California (PANW headquarters). Legal counsel is Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz.
L3_detailed.plan_details: All six CyberArk equity compensation plans are referenced. No new plan terms are disclosed in this filing as it solely effects deregistration. The 15 original S-8 filings span from November 2014 through March 2025.
risks_flagged: Post-merger deregistration filing confirms deal closure — CyberArk ordinary shares are no longer being offered under any employee benefit plan
The exact number of shares that remained unsold (and are now deregistered) is not disclosed in the filing
All CyberArk standalone equity compensation plans have been terminated as a consequence of the merger
